name: blog_authorship
description: Blog Authorship Corpus (Schler et al. 2006), gender contrast. target = female bloggers (y=1), baseline = male bloggers (y=0); x = a <=120-word segment of one blog post. Non-commercial research use only.

cohorts.name: null_split_a
cohorts.display_name: Female bloggers, random split A (null control)
cohorts.description: Seeded random split of held-out female authors (disjoint from target); same distribution as split B.

cohorts.name: null_split_b
cohorts.display_name: Female bloggers, random split B (null control)
cohorts.description: Seeded random split of held-out female authors (disjoint from target); same distribution as split A.

comparisons.name: target_vs_baseline
comparisons.notes: x-unit = one POST SEGMENT (first <=120 words of a post). Published 80.1% (Schler et al. 2006) is per-author over full posts, so expect lower per-prompt separability; per-author aggregation is the fair comparison. Attribution of any signal to TOPIC (industry/age themes, e.g. teens writing about school) vs STYLE is itself the finding — read the topical JSD alongside lexical/syntactic.

comparisons.name: null_control
comparisons.notes: Negative control: a seeded random author split within the female group. Expect C2ST ~= 0.5, no BH survivors, null MMD. Any strong signal here indicates a pipeline defect.
